The user wants me to check if the devcontainer setup is ready for manual running. Let me check the key devcontainer files and configuration.Everything looks good! Here's the devcontainer setup status:

## ✅ Devcontainer Ready for Manual Setup

| Component | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| **`devcontainer.json`** | ✅ Ready | Auto-run disabled, Node 22 + pnpm 9 |
| **`docker-compose.yml`** | ✅ Ready | 3 services: app, postgres, redis |
| **Dockerfile** | ✅ Ready | TypeScript/Node image with tools |
| **`post-create.sh`** | ✅ Available | For manual setup if needed |
| **post-start.sh** | ✅ Available | For manual service checks |

### To Open in Devcontainer:
1. Press **F1** → **"Dev Containers: Reopen in Container"**
2. Wait for container to build (~2-3 min first time)

### Manual Setup Commands (after container opens):
```bash
# 1. Install dependencies
pnpm install

# 2. Generate Prisma client
pnpm db:generate

# 3. Push database schema
pnpm db:push

# 4. Seed database
pnpm db:seed

# 5. Start development servers
pnpm dev
```

### Default Login:
- **Email:** admin@nucleus.local
- **Password:** admin123

The devcontainer will automatically start PostgreSQL and Redis - you just need to run the setup commands manually.

{
  "kind": "terminal",
  "terminalToolSessionId": "074b76c5-78ec-4c07-984e-c87ad72e3a20",

  "commandLine": {
    "original": "cat &gt; /home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/1_COMPANY_DEV/CompanyApps/Nucleus-Business-Suite/README.md &lt;&lt; 'ENDOFFILE'\n# Nucleus Business Suite\n\nWeb-based ERP with multi-platform POS system built with NestJS, Next.js, and Tauri.\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Backend:** NestJS (TypeScript)\n- **Web Frontend:** Next.js 14 (React, TypeScript)\n- **Desktop/Mobile POS:** Tauri v2 (React + Rust)\n- **Database:** PostgreSQL (via Prisma)\n- **Cache:** Redis\n- **File Storage:** S3-compatible (Backblaze B2)\n- **Monorepo:** pnpm + Turborepo\n\n## Prerequisites\n\n- Node.js &gt;= 20.0.0\n- pnpm &gt;= 9.0.0\n- Docker &amp; Docker Compose\n- Rust (for Tauri POS development - optional)\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Quick Start\n\n### 1. Clone and Install\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ngit clone https://github.com/yourcompany/nucleus-business-suite.git\ncd nucleus-business-suite\npnpm install\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 2. Setup Environment\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Create API environment file\ncat &gt; apps/api/.env &lt;&lt; 'EOF'\nDATABASE_URL=\"postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/nucleus?schema=public\"\nREDIS_URL=\"redis://localhost:6379\"\nJWT_SECRET=\"dev-secret-change-in-production\"\nJWT_EXPIRES_IN=\"15m\"\nREFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRES_IN=\"7d\"\nPORT=4000\nCORS_ORIGINS=\"http://localhost:3000\"\nEOF\n\n# Create Web environment file\ncat &gt; apps/web/.env.local &lt;&lt; 'EOF'\nNEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=\"http://localhost:4000\"\nEOF\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 3. Start Docker Services (PostgreSQL &amp; Redis)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npnpm docker:dev\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis starts PostgreSQL and Redis in Docker containers.\n\n### 4. Setup Database\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npnpm db:generate  # Generate Prisma client\npnpm db:push      # Push schema to database\npnpm db:seed      # Seed initial data\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 5. Start Development\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npnpm dev\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 6. Access the Application\n\n| Service           | URL                        |\n| ----------------- | -------------------------- |\n| **Web App**       | http://localhost:3000      |\n| **API**           | http://localhost:4000      |\n| **API Docs**      | http://localhost:4000/docs |\n| **Prisma Studio** | Run \\&#96;pnpm db:studio\\&#96;       |\n\n---\n\n## 🔑 Default Credentials\n\n| User Type       | Email                 | Password   |\n| --------------- | --------------------- | ---------- |\n| **Super Admin** | \\&#96;admin@nucleus.local\\&#96; | \\&#96;admin123\\&#96; |\n\n---\n\n## 🐳 Docker Commands\n\n### Development Services\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Start PostgreSQL &amp; Redis\npnpm docker:dev\n\n# Stop services\npnpm docker:dev:down\n\n# View logs\ndocker compose --profile local logs -f\n\n# Check service status\ndocker compose --profile local ps\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Database Management\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Generate Prisma client (after schema changes)\npnpm db:generate\n\n# Push schema to database (development)\npnpm db:push\n\n# Create migration (production)\npnpm db:migrate\n\n# Seed database\npnpm db:seed\n\n# Open Prisma Studio (database GUI)\npnpm db:studio\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Project Structure\n\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n├── apps/\n│   ├── api/              # NestJS backend\n│   ├── web/              # Next.js frontend (dashboard + eCommerce)\n│   └── pos/              # Tauri POS application\n├── packages/\n│   ├── shared/           # Shared types &amp; utilities\n│   └── ui/               # Shared UI components (DaisyUI)\n├── docker/               # Docker configurations for deployment\n├── scripts/              # Build &amp; deployment scripts\n├── docker-compose.yml    # Local development services\n└── docs/                 # Documentation\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n\n## Architecture Principles\n\n### 🧩 Modularity First\n\nEvery feature is built as a **self-contained module**:\n\n- ✅ Easy to add new features without modifying core code\n- ✅ Features can be enabled/disabled independently\n- ✅ Clear separation of concerns\n- ✅ Reusable and testable components\n\n**📖 See [Module Creation Guide](docs/MODULE_CREATION.md) for detailed instructions on creating new modules**\n\n### 🔄 Environment-Based Configuration\n\n- **Test (local):** PostgreSQL and Redis in Docker\n- **Staging:** External cloud services (Neon, Upstash, B2)\n- **Production:** Same as staging with different credentials\n- All controlled via environment variables - NO code changes\n\n---\n\n## Development Commands\n\n| Command                | Description                        |\n| ---------------------- | ---------------------------------- |\n| \\&#96;pnpm dev\\&#96;             | Start all apps in development mode |\n| \\&#96;pnpm build\\&#96;           | Build all apps                     |\n| \\&#96;pnpm test\\&#96;            | Run tests                          |\n| \\&#96;pnpm lint\\&#96;            | Lint all packages                  |\n| \\&#96;pnpm docker:dev\\&#96;      | Start local PostgreSQL &amp; Redis     |\n| \\&#96;pnpm docker:dev:down\\&#96; | Stop local services                |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:generate\\&#96;     | Generate Prisma client             |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:push\\&#96;         | Push schema to database            |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:migrate\\&#96;      | Create database migration          |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:seed\\&#96;         | Seed initial data                  |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:studio\\&#96;       | Open Prisma Studio (database GUI)  |\n\n---\n\n## VS Code Tasks\n\nIf using VS Code, you can run common tasks via \\&#96;Ctrl+Shift+P\\&#96; → \"Tasks: Run Task\":\n\n- **Dev: Start All** - Start API + Web servers\n- **Dev: Start API** - Start only API server\n- **Dev: Start Web** - Start only Web server\n- **DB: Push Schema** - Push Prisma schema\n- **DB: Open Studio** - Open Prisma Studio\n\n---\n\n## Environments\n\n| Environment | Branch    | URL                       |\n| ----------- | --------- | ------------------------- |\n| Test        | \\&#96;develop\\&#96; | \\&#96;localhost\\&#96;               |\n| Staging     | \\&#96;staging\\&#96; | \\&#96;staging.yourcompany.com\\&#96; |\n| Production  | \\&#96;main\\&#96;    | \\&#96;app.yourcompany.com\\&#96;     |\n\n---\n\n## License\n\nProprietary - All rights reserved\nENDOFFILE"
  },

  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# NUCLEUS BUSINESS SUITE - LOCAL DEVELOPMENT\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# Usage:\n#   Start local DB &amp; Redis:  docker compose --profile local up -d\n#   Stop local services:     docker compose --profile local down\n#   View logs:               docker compose --profile local logs -f\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\nversion: '3.8'\n\nservices:\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # LOCAL DATABASE (Test environment only)\n  # In Staging/Production, use external service (Neon, Supabase, RDS)\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  postgres:\n    image: postgres:16-alpine\n    profiles: [\"local\"]\n    container_name: nucleus-postgres\n    environment:\n      POSTGRES_USER: postgres\n      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres\n      POSTGRES_DB: nucleus\n    ports:\n      - \"5432:5432\"\n    volumes:\n      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD-SHELL\", \"pg_isready -U postgres\"]\n      interval: 10s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 5\n\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # LOCAL REDIS (Test environment only)\n  # In Staging/Production, use external service (Upstash)\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  redis:\n    image: redis:7-alpine\n    profiles: [\"local\"]\n    container_name: nucleus-redis\n    ports:\n      - \"6379:6379\"\n    volumes:\n      - redis_data:/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD\", \"redis-cli\", \"ping\"]\n      interval: 10s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 5\n    command: redis-server --appendonly yes\n\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # LOCAL MINIO (Optional - S3-compatible storage for testing)\n  # Uncomment if you want to test S3 locally instead of Backblaze\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # minio:\n  #   image: minio/minio\n  #   profiles: [\"local\"]\n  #   container_name: nucleus-minio\n  #   environment:\n  #     MINIO_ROOT_USER: minioadmin\n  #     MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: minioadmin\n  #   ports:\n  #     - \"9000:9000\"\n  #     - \"9001:9001\"\n  #   volumes:\n  #     - minio_data:/data\n  #   command: server /data --console-address \":9001\"\n\nvolumes:\n  postgres_data:\n  redis_data:\n  # minio_data:\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}

  "textEditContent": "# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# NUCLEUS BUSINESS SUITE - DEPLOYMENT DOCKER COMPOSE\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# Usage:\n#   Build images:  docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.deploy.yml build\n#   Deploy:        docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.deploy.yml up -d\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\nversion: '3.8'\n\nservices:\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # API Server\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  api:\n    build:\n      context: ..\n      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.api\n    container_name: nucleus-api\n    environment:\n      - NODE_ENV=production\n      - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL}\n      - REDIS_URL=${REDIS_URL}\n      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}\n      - CORS_ORIGINS=${CORS_ORIGINS}\n    ports:\n      - \"4000:4000\"\n    restart: unless-stopped\n\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Web Frontend\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  web:\n    build:\n      context: ..\n      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.web\n    container_name: nucleus-web\n    environment:\n      - NODE_ENV=production\n      - NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=${NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL}\n    ports:\n      - \"3000:3000\"\n    depends_on:\n      - api\n    restart: unless-stopped\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}

Parameters:

```json
{
  "kind": "terminal",
  "terminalToolSessionId": "a423f294-9403-43f5-8257-fa3e3852aa10",
  "terminalCommandId": "tool-3c583cb1-3f2f-4478-b6cb-329f3e19d13e",
  "commandLine": {
    "original": "cat &gt; /home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/1_COMPANY_DEV/CompanyApps/Nucleus-Business-Suite/README.md &lt;&lt; 'ENDOFFILE'\n# Nucleus Business Suite\n\nWeb-based ERP with multi-platform POS system built with NestJS, Next.js, and Tauri.\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Backend:** NestJS (TypeScript)\n- **Web Frontend:** Next.js 14 (React, TypeScript)\n- **Desktop/Mobile POS:** Tauri v2 (React + Rust)\n- **Database:** PostgreSQL (via Prisma)\n- **Cache:** Redis\n- **File Storage:** S3-compatible (Backblaze B2)\n- **Monorepo:** pnpm + Turborepo\n\n## Prerequisites\n\n- Node.js &gt;= 20.0.0\n- pnpm &gt;= 9.0.0\n- Docker &amp; Docker Compose\n- Rust (for Tauri POS development - optional)\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Quick Start\n\n### 1. Clone and Install\n\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;bash\ngit clone https://github.com/yourcompany/nucleus-business-suite.git\ncd nucleus-business-suite\npnpm install\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n\n### 2. Setup Environment\n\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;bash\n# Create API environment file\ncat &gt; apps/api/.env &lt;&lt; 'EOF'\nDATABASE_URL=\"postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/nucleus?schema=public\"\nREDIS_URL=\"redis://localhost:6379\"\nJWT_SECRET=\"dev-secret-change-in-production\"\nJWT_EXPIRES_IN=\"15m\"\nREFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRES_IN=\"7d\"\nPORT=4000\nCORS_ORIGINS=\"http://localhost:3000\"\nEOF\n\n# Create Web environment file\ncat &gt; apps/web/.env.local &lt;&lt; 'EOF'\nNEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=\"http://localhost:4000\"\nEOF\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n\n### 3. Start Docker Services (PostgreSQL &amp; Redis)\n\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;bash\npnpm docker:dev\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n\nThis starts PostgreSQL and Redis in Docker containers.\n\n### 4. Setup Database\n\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;bash\npnpm db:generate  # Generate Prisma client\npnpm db:push      # Push schema to database\npnpm db:seed      # Seed initial data\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n\n### 5. Start Development\n\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;bash\npnpm dev\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n\n### 6. Access the Application\n\n| Service           | URL                        |\n| ----------------- | -------------------------- |\n| **Web App**       | http://localhost:3000      |\n| **API**           | http://localhost:4000      |\n| **API Docs**      | http://localhost:4000/docs |\n| **Prisma Studio** | Run \\&#96;pnpm db:studio\\&#96;       |\n\n---\n\n## 🔑 Default Credentials\n\n| User Type       | Email                 | Password   |\n| --------------- | --------------------- | ---------- |\n| **Super Admin** | \\&#96;admin@nucleus.local\\&#96; | \\&#96;admin123\\&#96; |\n\n---\n\n## 🐳 Docker Commands\n\n### Development Services\n\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;bash\n# Start PostgreSQL &amp; Redis\npnpm docker:dev\n\n# Stop services\npnpm docker:dev:down\n\n# View logs\ndocker compose --profile local logs -f\n\n# Check service status\ndocker compose --profile local ps\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n\n### Database Management\n\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;bash\n# Generate Prisma client (after schema changes)\npnpm db:generate\n\n# Push schema to database (development)\npnpm db:push\n\n# Create migration (production)\npnpm db:migrate\n\n# Seed database\npnpm db:seed\n\n# Open Prisma Studio (database GUI)\npnpm db:studio\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Project Structure\n\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n├── apps/\n│   ├── api/              # NestJS backend\n│   ├── web/              # Next.js frontend (dashboard + eCommerce)\n│   └── pos/              # Tauri POS application\n├── packages/\n│   ├── shared/           # Shared types &amp; utilities\n│   └── ui/               # Shared UI components (DaisyUI)\n├── docker/               # Docker configurations for deployment\n├── scripts/              # Build &amp; deployment scripts\n├── docker-compose.yml    # Local development services\n└── docs/                 # Documentation\n\\&#96;\\&#96;\\&#96;\n\n## Architecture Principles\n\n### 🧩 Modularity First\n\nEvery feature is built as a **self-contained module**:\n\n- ✅ Easy to add new features without modifying core code\n- ✅ Features can be enabled/disabled independently\n- ✅ Clear separation of concerns\n- ✅ Reusable and testable components\n\n**📖 See [Module Creation Guide](docs/MODULE_CREATION.md) for detailed instructions on creating new modules**\n\n### 🔄 Environment-Based Configuration\n\n- **Test (local):** PostgreSQL and Redis in Docker\n- **Staging:** External cloud services (Neon, Upstash, B2)\n- **Production:** Same as staging with different credentials\n- All controlled via environment variables - NO code changes\n\n---\n\n## Development Commands\n\n| Command                | Description                        |\n| ---------------------- | ---------------------------------- |\n| \\&#96;pnpm dev\\&#96;             | Start all apps in development mode |\n| \\&#96;pnpm build\\&#96;           | Build all apps                     |\n| \\&#96;pnpm test\\&#96;            | Run tests                          |\n| \\&#96;pnpm lint\\&#96;            | Lint all packages                  |\n| \\&#96;pnpm docker:dev\\&#96;      | Start local PostgreSQL &amp; Redis     |\n| \\&#96;pnpm docker:dev:down\\&#96; | Stop local services                |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:generate\\&#96;     | Generate Prisma client             |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:push\\&#96;         | Push schema to database            |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:migrate\\&#96;      | Create database migration          |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:seed\\&#96;         | Seed initial data                  |\n| \\&#96;pnpm db:studio\\&#96;       | Open Prisma Studio (database GUI)  |\n\n---\n\n## VS Code Tasks\n\nIf using VS Code, you can run common tasks via \\&#96;Ctrl+Shift+P\\&#96; → \"Tasks: Run Task\":\n\n- **Dev: Start All** - Start API + Web servers\n- **Dev: Start API** - Start only API server\n- **Dev: Start Web** - Start only Web server\n- **DB: Push Schema** - Push Prisma schema\n- **DB: Open Studio** - Open Prisma Studio\n\n---\n\n## Environments\n\n| Environment | Branch    | URL                       |\n| ----------- | --------- | ------------------------- |\n| Test        | \\&#96;develop\\&#96; | \\&#96;localhost\\&#96;               |\n| Staging     | \\&#96;staging\\&#96; | \\&#96;staging.yourcompany.com\\&#96; |\n| Production  | \\&#96;main\\&#96;    | \\&#96;app.yourcompany.com\\&#96;     |\n\n---\n\n## License\n\nProprietary - All rights reserved\nENDOFFILE"
  },

The build history you're seeing in Docker Desktop might be **UI history/logs** that Docker Desktop keeps separately. To clear that:

1. Open **Docker Desktop**

3. Or try: **Troubleshoot** (bug icon) → **Clean / Purge data**

Alternatively, you can reset Docker Desktop completely:
- **Settings** → **Troubleshoot** → **Reset to factory defaults**

This is likely a DaisyUI version compatibility issue. Let me check and fix the tailwind config: The issue is that `text-base-content` is a DaisyUI class but DaisyUI 5.x has changed how themes work. Let me fix the `globals.css` to use standard Tailwind classes instead of DaisyUI-specific ones: There are more DaisyUI classes being used. The user is saying the page is blank but getting 200 status. This could be a client-side rendering issue or the page might be loading but not rendering properly. Let me check the terminal output for any errors and also look at what the main page component looks like.The server-side is working (200 OK), but the page is blank. This is likely a client-side JavaScript issue. Let me check the main page and layout: Found it! The layout is using DaisyUI classes (`bg-base-200`, `bg-base-100`) which don't exist in DaisyUI v5. Let me check the providers and fix the layout: Found the issue. Multiple places are using DaisyUI classes. Let me fix all of them: Now let me check for other DaisyUI class usages in the codebase: There are many files using DaisyUI classes. Let me run a quick search-and-replace to fix all of them: The class names have been replaced. Now let me clear the Next.js cache and restart the dev server: Both servers are running. Let me open the app in the browser so you can see it: The development servers are running:
- **Web**: http://localhost:3000
- **API**: http://localhost:5000
- **API Docs**: http://localhost:5000/docs

I've replaced all the incompatible DaisyUI v5 class names with standard Tailwind equivalents:
- `text-base-content` → `text-slate-900 dark:text-slate-100`
- `bg-base-100` → `bg-slate-900`
- `bg-base-200` → `bg-slate-100`
- `bg-base-300` → `bg-slate-200`
- `border-base-300` → `border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700`

Try refreshing the page now. You can log in with:
- **Email**: `admin@nucleus.local`
- **Password**: `admin123`

  "textEditContent": "# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# NUCLEUS BUSINESS SUITE - Development Container\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# This Dockerfile creates an isolated development environment with:\n# - Node.js 20 LTS\n# - pnpm package manager\n# - All necessary build tools\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\nFROM node:20-bookworm\n\n# Install essential tools\nRUN apt-get update &amp;&amp; apt-get install -y \\\n    git \\\n    curl \\\n    wget \\\n    zsh \\\n    openssh-client \\\n    # For Prisma\n    openssl \\\n    # For native modules\n    python3 \\\n    make \\\n    g++ \\\n    &amp;&amp; rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*\n\n# Install pnpm globally\nRUN corepack enable &amp;&amp; corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate\n\n# Install Turbo globally for monorepo commands\nRUN pnpm add -g turbo\n\n# Set up shell history for persistence\nRUN SNIPPET=\"export PROMPT_COMMAND='history -a' &amp;&amp; export HISTFILE=/commandhistory/.bash_history\" \\\n    &amp;&amp; mkdir -p /commandhistory \\\n    &amp;&amp; touch /commandhistory/.bash_history \\\n    &amp;&amp; chown -R node:node /commandhistory \\\n    &amp;&amp; echo \"$SNIPPET\" &gt;&gt; \"/home/node/.bashrc\"\n\n# Create workspace directory\nWORKDIR /workspace\n\n# Set ownership for node user\nRUN chown -R node:node /workspace\n\n# Switch to non-root user for better security\nUSER node\n\n# Expose development ports\nEXPOSE 3000 5000\n\n# Default command (overridden by devcontainer.json)\nCMD [\"sleep\", \"infinity\"]\n",

  "textEditContent": "# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# NUCLEUS BUSINESS SUITE - FULL DEVELOPMENT STACK\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# Run the entire application in Docker with hot-reload support\n#\n# Usage:\n#   Start all services:      docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d\n#   View logs:               docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f\n#   Stop all services:       docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down\n#   Rebuild after changes:   docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build\n#\n# Access:\n#   Web App:    http://localhost:3000\n#   API:        http://localhost:5000\n#   API Docs:   http://localhost:5000/docs\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\nservices:\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # PostgreSQL Database\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  postgres:\n    image: postgres:16-alpine\n    container_name: nucleus-postgres\n    environment:\n      POSTGRES_USER: postgres\n      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres\n      POSTGRES_DB: nucleus\n    ports:\n      - \"5432:5432\"\n    volumes:\n      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD-SHELL\", \"pg_isready -U postgres\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 5\n    networks:\n      - nucleus-network\n\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Redis Cache\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  redis:\n    image: redis:7-alpine\n    container_name: nucleus-redis\n    ports:\n      - \"6379:6379\"\n    volumes:\n      - redis_data:/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD\", \"redis-cli\", \"ping\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 5\n    networks:\n      - nucleus-network\n\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # NestJS API Server (Development with hot-reload)\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  api:\n    build:\n      context: .\n      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.api.dev\n    container_name: nucleus-api\n    volumes:\n      # Mount source code for hot-reload\n      - ./apps/api:/app/apps/api:cached\n      - ./packages:/app/packages:cached\n      - ./package.json:/app/package.json:ro\n      - ./pnpm-workspace.yaml:/app/pnpm-workspace.yaml:ro\n      - ./pnpm-lock.yaml:/app/pnpm-lock.yaml:ro\n      - NODE_ENV=development\n      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/nucleus?schema=public\n      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379\n      - JWT_SECRET=dev-secret-key-change-in-production\n      - JWT_REFRESH_SECRET=dev-refresh-secret-change-in-production\n      - PORT=5000\n    depends_on:\n      postgres:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      redis:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    networks:\n      - nucleus-network\n\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Next.js Web App (Development with hot-reload)\n  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  web:\n    build:\n      context: .\n      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.web.dev\n    container_name: nucleus-web\n    volumes:\n      # Mount source code for hot-reload\n      - ./apps/web:/app/apps/web:cached\n      - ./packages:/app/packages:cached\n      - ./package.json:/app/package.json:ro\n      - ./pnpm-workspace.yaml:/app/pnpm-workspace.yaml:ro\n      - ./pnpm-lock.yaml:/app/pnpm-lock.yaml:ro\n      -   "textEditContent": "# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# NestJS API - Development Dockerfile\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# Hot-reload enabled development container for the API\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\nFROM node:20-alpine\n\n# Install dependencies for Prisma and native modules\nRUN apk add --no-cache \\\n    openssl \\\n    libc6-compat \\\n    python3 \\\n    make \\\n    g++\n\n# Enable pnpm\nRUN corepack enable &amp;&amp; corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate\n\nWORKDIR /app\n\n# Copy package files first for better caching\nCOPY package.json pnpm-workspace.yaml pnpm-lock.yaml turbo.json ./\nCOPY apps/api/package.json ./apps/api/\nCOPY packages/shared/package.json ./packages/shared/\nCOPY packages/ui/package.json ./packages/ui/\n\n# Install dependencies\nRUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile\n\n# Copy Prisma schema and generate client\nCOPY apps/api/prisma ./apps/api/prisma\nRUN pnpm --filter @nucleus/api exec prisma generate\n\n# Copy source code (will be overwritten by volume mount in dev)\nCOPY apps/api ./apps/api\nCOPY packages ./packages\n\n# Expose API port\nEXPOSE 5000\n\n# Start development server with hot-reload\nCMD [\"pnpm\", \"--filter\", \"@nucleus/api\", \"dev\"]\n",

  "textEditContent": "# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# Next.js Web - Development Dockerfile\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# Hot-reload enabled development container for the Web app\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\nFROM node:20-alpine\n\n# Install dependencies for native modules\nRUN apk add --no-cache \\\n    libc6-compat \\\n    python3 \\\n    make \\\n    g++\n\n# Enable pnpm\nRUN corepack enable &amp;&amp; corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate\n\nWORKDIR /app\n\n# Copy package files first for better caching\nCOPY package.json pnpm-workspace.yaml pnpm-lock.yaml turbo.json ./\nCOPY apps/web/package.json ./apps/web/\nCOPY packages/shared/package.json ./packages/shared/\nCOPY packages/ui/package.json ./packages/ui/\n\n# Install dependencies\nRUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile\n\n# Copy source code (will be overwritten by volume mount in dev)\nCOPY apps/web ./apps/web\nCOPY packages ./packages\n\n# Expose Next.js port\nEXPOSE 3000\n\n# Enable file watching in Docker (needed for hot-reload on some systems)\nENV WATCHPACK_POLLING=true\nENV CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING=true\n\n# Start development server with hot-reload\nCMD [\"pnpm\", \"--filter\", \"@nucleus/web\", \"dev\"]\n",

{
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/1_COMPANY_DEV/CompanyApps/Nucleus-Business-Suite/docs/DEVELOPMENT.md)",

  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "# Nucleus Business Suite - Development Environment\n\nThis guide explains how to run the development environment **completely inside Docker** without installing any runtimes on your local machine.\n\n## 🎯 Two Options for Isolated Development\n\n### Option 1: VS Code Dev Containers (Recommended)\n\nThis gives you the best experience with full IDE support inside the container.\n\n**Prerequisites:**\n- [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/) (or Docker Engine + Docker Compose)\n- [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/)\n- [Dev Containers extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers)\n\n**Getting Started:**\n\n1. Open VS Code in this folder\n2. Press &#96;F1&#96; → &#96;Dev Containers: Reopen in Container&#96;\n3. Wait for the container to build (first time takes ~2-5 minutes)\n4. Once inside, open the terminal and run:\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n   pnpm dev\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\n5. Access the apps:\n   - **Web App**: http://localhost:3000\n   - **API**: http://localhost:5000\n   - **API Docs**: http://localhost:5000/docs\n\n**What happens:**\n- VS Code opens inside a Docker container with Node.js 20 + pnpm pre-installed\n- PostgreSQL and Redis containers start automatically\n- Your code is mounted into the container (changes sync instantly)\n- All VS Code extensions work inside the container\n\n---\n\n### Option 2: Docker Compose Full Stack\n\nRun everything in Docker containers without VS Code Dev Containers.\n\n**Prerequisites:**\n- [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/) (or Docker Engine + Docker Compose)\n\n**Getting Started:**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Start the full development stack\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d\n\n# View logs (all services)\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f\n\n# View logs (specific service)\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f api\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f web\n\n# Run database migrations (first time setup)\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml exec api pnpm db:push\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml exec api pnpm db:seed\n\n# Stop all services\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down\n\n# Rebuild after Dockerfile changes\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build\n\n# Full reset (removes volumes)\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down -v\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Access:**\n- **Web App**: http://localhost:3000\n- **API**: http://localhost:5000\n- **API Docs**: http://localhost:5000/docs\n\n---\n\n## 📁 Development Container Structure\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n.devcontainer/\n├── devcontainer.json      # VS Code Dev Container config\n├── docker-compose.dev.yml # Override for dev container\n└── Dockerfile             # Development container image\n\ndocker/\n├── Dockerfile.api.dev     # API development image\n├── Dockerfile.web.dev     # Web development image\n└── docker-compose.deploy.yml # Production deployment\n\ndocker-compose.yml         # Base services (PostgreSQL, Redis)\ndocker-compose.dev.yml     # Full development stack\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## 🔧 Available Scripts\n\nFrom inside the dev container (or your host if you have pnpm):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Development\npnpm dev              # Start all apps in dev mode\n\n# Database\npnpm db:generate      # Generate Prisma client\npnpm db:push          # Push schema to database\npnpm db:seed          # Seed database with sample data\npnpm db:studio        # Open Prisma Studio\n\n# Docker (run from host)\npnpm docker:local     # Start only PostgreSQL + Redis\npnpm docker:dev       # Start full development stack\npnpm docker:dev:logs  # View all container logs\npnpm docker:dev:build # Rebuild containers\npnpm docker:dev:reset # Full reset with volume cleanup\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## 🔄 Hot Reload\n\nBoth options support hot-reload:\n- **API (NestJS)**: Changes to &#96;apps/api/src&#96; auto-reload the server\n- **Web (Next.js)**: Changes to &#96;apps/web/src&#96; auto-reload the browser\n- **Shared packages**: Changes to &#96;packages/*&#96; trigger rebuilds\n\n---\n\n## 🗄️ Database Access\n\n**Connection string:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npostgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/nucleus\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Via Prisma Studio:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Inside dev container or with local pnpm\npnpm db:studio\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Via psql (if you have it locally):**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ndocker exec -it nucleus-postgres psql -U postgres -d nucleus\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## 🧹 Cleanup\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Stop and remove containers (keeps data)\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down\n\n# Stop and remove containers + volumes (deletes data)\ndocker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down -v\n\n# Remove all project images\ndocker rmi $(docker images 'nucleus*' -q)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## 🐛 Troubleshooting\n\n### Hot reload not working?\n- Make sure &#96;WATCHPACK_POLLING=true&#96; is set (it's in the docker-compose)\n- Try rebuilding: &#96;docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build&#96;\n\n### Port already in use?\n- Check what's using the port: &#96;lsof -i :3000&#96;\n- Stop the conflicting service or change the port in docker-compose.dev.yml\n\n### Container won't start?\n- Check logs: &#96;docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs api&#96;\n- Try a full reset: &#96;docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down -v&#96;\n\n### Permission issues?\n- The containers run as non-root user for security\n- Make sure your source files have correct permissions\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

---

The user wants to avoid installing runtimes and libraries directly on their WSL2 system to keep it clean and prevent conflicts between projects. They want a fully isolated Docker-based development environment where they can run the entire Nucleus Business Suite in development mode inside Docker containers.

This is a common pattern called "Dev Containers" or "Docker-based development". There are a few approaches:

1. **VS Code Dev Containers** - Use the devcontainer.json to run the entire development environment inside a container. VS Code connects to the container and all commands run inside it.

2. **Docker Compose for Development** - Create a docker-compose that runs the app in development mode with hot-reload, mounting the source code as volumes.

For this project (monorepo with NestJS API, Next.js web, and Tauri POS), the best approach would be:

1. Create a development Dockerfile that has all the necessary runtimes (Node.js, pnpm)
2. Create a docker-compose.dev.yml that:
   - Runs PostgreSQL and Redis (already have this)
   - Runs the API in development mode with hot-reload
   - Runs the Web in development mode with hot-reload
   - Mounts the source code as volumes so changes are reflected immediately

3. Optionally set up VS Code Dev Containers so the user can develop entirely inside the container with VS Code

Let me suggest this approach and create the necessary files.Great point! Running everything directly on WSL2 can clutter your system with different Node versions, Python runtimes, etc. Here's the best solution for isolated development:

## Recommended: VS Code Dev Containers + Docker Compose

This approach gives you:
1. **Complete isolation** - All runtimes (Node.js, pnpm) run inside containers
2. **Hot-reload** - Code changes reflect immediately via volume mounts
3. **Consistent environment** - Same setup for all team members
4. **VS Code integration** - Full IntelliSense, debugging, extensions inside the container

Let me create the necessary configuration: Now let me create the Dev Container configuration and a development Docker Compose setup: Now let me also create a standalone Docker Compose for development (in case you want to run it without VS Code Dev Containers): Now let me update the `package.json` with convenient scripts: Now let me create a helpful README for the development setup: I've created a complete isolated development setup. Here's a summary:

## 🐳 Isolated Docker Development - Two Options

### **Option 1: VS Code Dev Containers** (Recommended)
Your VS Code opens *inside* a Docker container with everything pre-installed:

1. Install the **Dev Containers** extension in VS Code
2. Press `F1` → `Dev Containers: Reopen in Container`
3. Wait for build (~2-5 min first time)
4. Run `pnpm dev` in the terminal

**Benefits:**
- Full IntelliSense, debugging, extensions work inside container
- No Node.js, pnpm, or any runtime on your WSL2
- Just Docker + VS Code needed

---

### **Option 2: Docker Compose Full Stack**
Run everything in containers without opening VS Code inside them:

```bash
# Start the full stack (API + Web + PostgreSQL + Redis)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d

# View logs
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f

# First-time database setup
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml exec api pnpm db:push
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml exec api pnpm db:seed
```

---

## 📁 Files Created

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `devcontainer.json` | VS Code Dev Container config |
| `Dockerfile` | Dev container image |
| `docker-compose.dev.yml` | Override for VS Code |
| `docker-compose.dev.yml` | Standalone full dev stack |
| `Dockerfile.api.dev` | API dev image with hot-reload |
| `Dockerfile.web.dev` | Web dev image with hot-reload |
| `DEVELOPMENT.md` | Full documentation |

Both options support **hot-reload** - edit files locally and see changes instantly in the containers.

Would you like me to stop the current dev servers and start the Docker-based development environment instead?

_**Agent (model copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is asking about whether both options (VS Code Dev Containers vs Docker Compose Full Stack) offer the same benefits, particularly for debugging errors. Let me clarify the differences honestly.Good question - **No, they're not equal**. Here's an honest comparison:

## 🔍 Key Differences

| Feature | **Option 1: Dev Containers** | **Option 2: Docker Compose** |
|---------|------------------------------|------------------------------|
| **Debugging with breakpoints** | ✅ Full support - F5, breakpoints, step-through | ⚠️ Limited - need manual setup for remote debugging |
| **IntelliSense/Autocomplete** | ✅ Full - VS Code runs inside container | ⚠️ Partial - may miss container-specific paths |
| **Go to Definition** | ✅ Works perfectly | ⚠️ May not resolve node_modules correctly |
| **Error highlighting** | ✅ Real-time, accurate | ⚠️ May show false errors (path mismatches) |
| **Extensions** | ✅ Run inside container | ❌ Run on host, can't see container context |
| **Terminal** | ✅ Already inside container | 🔄 Need `docker exec` to get inside |
| **File watching** | ✅ Native, fast | ⚠️ Polling-based, slightly slower |
| **Setup complexity** | Medium (one-time) | Simple |
| **Resource usage** | Higher (VS Code server in container) | Lower |

---

## 🐛 Debugging Comparison

### Dev Containers (Option 1)
```
- Set breakpoint in VS Code → Hit F5 → Debugger stops at line
- Full variable inspection, call stack, watch expressions
- Works exactly like local development
```

### Docker Compose (Option 2)
```
- Need to configure remote debugging manually
- Add debug flags to container: --inspect=0.0.0.0:9229
- Attach VS Code debugger to remote port
- More complex, easier to break
```

---

## 🎯 My Recommendation

| Use Case | Best Option |
|----------|-------------|
| **Active development** | Dev Containers |
| **Just running/testing** | Docker Compose |
| **CI/CD pipelines** | Docker Compose |
| **Quick demo** | Docker Compose |
| **Team with mixed editors** | Docker Compose |

---

**Bottom line**: If you want the full debugging experience (breakpoints, step-through, variable inspection) without hassle, **Dev Containers is significantly better**. Docker Compose is great for running the stack, but debugging requires extra configuration.

Would you like me to set up remote debugging for the Docker Compose option as well, so you have both fully functional?

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="copilot_applyPatch">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **copilot_applyPatch** • Using "Apply Patch"</summary>

```yaml
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DEV CONTAINER - Docker Compose Override
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# This file extends docker-compose.yml for VS Code Dev Containers
# All development happens inside the 'app' container
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

services:
  app:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: .devcontainer/Dockerfile
    init: true
    tty: true
    stdin_open: true
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for hot-reload
      - ..:/workspace:cached
      # Persist node_modules inside container (faster than mounting from host)
      - node_modules:/workspace/node_modules
      - pnpm_store:/workspace/.pnpm-store
      # Persist shell history
      - bash_history:/commandhistory
    
    # Keep container running
    command: sleep infinity
    
    # Connect to the same network as postgres/redis
    networks:
      - default
    
    # Environment for development
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=development
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/nucleus?schema=public
      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
      - JWT_SECRET=dev-secret-key-change-in-production
      - JWT_REFRESH_SECRET=dev-refresh-secret-change-in-production
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:5000
    
    # Wait for services to be ready
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy

  # Override postgres to always start (remove profile requirement)
  postgres:
    profiles: []
  
  # Override redis to always start (remove profile requirement)
  redis:
    profiles: []

volumes:
  node_modules:
  pnpm_store:
  bash_history:

```


</details>
</tool-use>

**Planning devcontainer stability fixes**

The main suspect for VS Code reconnect loops is container restarts caused by heavy post-create commands or insufficient resources. I'll read and patch the devcontainer compose file to add `restart: unless-stopped`, `init: true`, `stdin_open: true`, and `tty: true` on the app service to improve container resilience and prevent disconnect loops.**Enhancing container resilience**

Planning to add `init: true`, `tty: true`, `stdin_open: true`, and `restart: unless-stopped` near the command in docker-compose to improve container reconnection resilience. I'll also patch `.devcontainer/docker-compose.dev.yml` and keep post-create commands minimal to avoid repeated restarts, with guidance on manual rebuilds if needed.**Summarizing devcontainer.json updates**

The app service in devcontainer.json was enhanced with init, tty, stdin_open, and restart settings (lines 10–28) to keep the container alive and reduce VS Code disconnection issues. Users should rebuild the devcontainer via "Dev Containers: Rebuild Container" to apply changes and avoid running `docker compose down` directly on the host, as that disrupts the app service. Additional tips include limiting watchers and checking Docker resources or container logs if connection loops persist.Changes made to stabilize Dev Containers:
- Added `init`, `tty`, `stdin_open`, and `restart: unless-stopped` to the devcontainer app service so the container stays alive and auto-recovers if a command exits or crashes (`undefined`).

What to do now:
- Reopen in container via `Dev Containers: Rebuild Container` (so the new compose settings are applied).

Operational tips to avoid the reconnect loop:
- Don’t run `docker compose down` on the devcontainer project; let VS Code manage it or use `Dev Containers: Rebuild`.
- Run commands inside the container terminal (not host) so the app service isn’t replaced.
- If it ever loops again, check logs: `docker compose -f `docker-compose.dev.yml` logs app`.

If you still hit disconnects after the rebuild, tell me the Docker/WSL2 resource limits (CPU/memory) and I’ll tune them.

If you want to reset just this project’s containers (and nothing else), do this:

1) Stop the VS Code Dev Container first (Close Remote Connection).
2) From the repo root, run:
```
docker compose -f .devcontainer/docker-compose.dev.yml down -v
```
This removes the devcontainer app + its volumes. It won’t touch other stacks if they use different project names/compose files.

Then reopen with “Dev Containers: Rebuild Container”.

If you have other project containers you care about, skip the `down -v` and just rebuild; otherwise a clean reset is fine. Need me to run the cleanup for you?

```markdown
# Nucleus Business Suite - Development Environment

This guide explains how to run the development environment **completely inside Docker** without installing any runtimes on your local machine.

## 🎯 Two Options for Isolated Development

### Option 1: VS Code Dev Containers (Recommended)

This gives you the best experience with full IDE support inside the container.

**Prerequisites:**
- [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/) (or Docker Engine + Docker Compose)
- [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/)
- [Dev Containers extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers)

**Getting Started:**

1. Open VS Code in this folder
2. Press `F1` → `Dev Containers: Reopen in Container`
3. Wait for the container to build (first time takes ~2-5 minutes)
4. Once inside, open the terminal and run:
   ```bash
   pnpm dev
   ```
5. Access the apps:
   - **Web App**: http://localhost:3000
   - **API**: http://localhost:5000
   - **API Docs**: http://localhost:5000/docs

**What happens:**
- VS Code opens inside a Docker container with Node.js 20 + pnpm pre-installed
- PostgreSQL and Redis containers start automatically
- Your code is mounted into the container (changes sync instantly)
- All VS Code extensions work inside the container

---

### Option 2: Docker Compose Full Stack

Run everything in Docker containers without VS Code Dev Containers.

**Prerequisites:**
- [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/) (or Docker Engine + Docker Compose)

**Getting Started:**

```bash
# Start the full development stack
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d

# Start with base + devcontainer overrides (good default for everyone)
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f .devcontainer/docker-compose.dev.yml --profile local up -d

# View logs (all services)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f

# View logs (specific service)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f api
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f web

# Run database migrations (first time setup)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml exec api pnpm db:push
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml exec api pnpm db:seed

# Stop all services
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down

# Rebuild after Dockerfile changes
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build

# Full reset (removes volumes)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down -v
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build
```

**Access:**
- **Web App**: http://localhost:3000
- **API**: http://localhost:5000
- **API Docs**: http://localhost:5000/docs

---

## 📁 Development Container Structure

```
.devcontainer/
├── devcontainer.json      # VS Code Dev Container config
├── docker-compose.dev.yml # Override for dev container
└── Dockerfile             # Development container image

docker/
├── Dockerfile.api.dev     # API development image
├── Dockerfile.web.dev     # Web development image
└── docker-compose.deploy.yml # Production deployment

docker-compose.yml         # Base services (PostgreSQL, Redis)
docker-compose.dev.yml     # Full development stack
```

---

## 🔧 Available Scripts

From inside the dev container (or your host if you have pnpm):

```bash
# Development
pnpm dev              # Start all apps in dev mode

# Database
pnpm db:generate      # Generate Prisma client
pnpm db:push          # Push schema to database
pnpm db:seed          # Seed database with sample data
pnpm db:studio        # Open Prisma Studio

# Docker (run from host)
pnpm docker:local     # Start only PostgreSQL + Redis
pnpm docker:dev       # Start full development stack
pnpm docker:dev:logs  # View all container logs
pnpm docker:dev:build # Rebuild containers
pnpm docker:dev:reset # Full reset with volume cleanup
```

---

## 🔄 Hot Reload

Both options support hot-reload:
- **API (NestJS)**: Changes to `apps/api/src` auto-reload the server
- **Web (Next.js)**: Changes to `apps/web/src` auto-reload the browser
- **Shared packages**: Changes to `packages/*` trigger rebuilds

---

## 🗄️ Database Access

**Connection string:**
```
postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/nucleus
```

**Via Prisma Studio:**
```bash
# Inside dev container or with local pnpm
pnpm db:studio
```

**Via psql (if you have it locally):**
```bash
docker exec -it nucleus-postgres psql -U postgres -d nucleus
```

---

## 🧹 Cleanup

```bash
# Stop and remove containers (keeps data)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down

# Stop and remove containers + volumes (deletes data)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down -v

# Remove all project images
docker rmi $(docker images 'nucleus*' -q)
```

---

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

### Hot reload not working?
- Make sure `WATCHPACK_POLLING=true` is set (it's in the docker-compose)
- Try rebuilding: `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build`

### Port already in use?
- Check what's using the port: `lsof -i :3000`
- Stop the conflicting service or change the port in docker-compose.dev.yml

### Container won't start?
- Check logs: `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs api`
- Try a full reset: `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down -v`

### Permission issues?
- The containers run as non-root user for security
- Make sure your source files have correct permissions

```


</details>
</tool-use>

To rebuild the Dev Container now:
1. Close the current container session if open.
2. In VS Code: `F1` → “Dev Containers: Rebuild Container”.
3. Wait for the build, then run inside the container terminal:
   - `pnpm dev` to start everything, or
   - `pnpm docker:local` if you just need Postgres/Redis (not necessary if `pnpm dev` suffices).

If you want, I can also kick off the `pnpm dev` inside the container once it’s rebuilt—just let me know.

This guide explains how to run the development environment **completely inside Docker** without installing any runtimes on your local machine.

## 🎯 Two Options for Isolated Development

### Option 1: VS Code Dev Containers (Recommended)

This gives you the best experience with full IDE support inside the container.

**Prerequisites:**
- [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/) (or Docker Engine + Docker Compose)
- [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/)
- [Dev Containers extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers)

**Getting Started:**

1. Open VS Code in this folder
2. Press `F1` → `Dev Containers: Reopen in Container`
3. Wait for the container to build (first time takes ~2-5 minutes)
4. Once inside, open the terminal and run:
   ```bash

5. Access the apps:
   - **Web App**: http://localhost:3000
   - **API**: http://localhost:5000
   - **API Docs**: http://localhost:5000/docs

**What happens:**

---

### Option 2: Docker Compose Full Stack

Run everything in Docker containers without VS Code Dev Containers.

**Prerequisites:**
- [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/) (or Docker Engine + Docker Compose)

**Getting Started:**

```bash
# Start the full development stack
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d

# Start with base + devcontainer overrides (good default for everyone)
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f .devcontainer/docker-compose.dev.yml --profile local up -d

# View logs (all services)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f

# View logs (specific service)

docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f web

docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml exec api pnpm db:seed

# Stop all services
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down

# Rebuild after Dockerfile changes
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build

# Full reset (removes volumes)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down -v
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build
```

**Access:**
- **Web App**: http://localhost:3000
- **API**: http://localhost:5000
- **API Docs**: http://localhost:5000/docs

---

## 📁 Development Container Structure

```
.devcontainer/
├── devcontainer.json      # VS Code Dev Container config
├── docker-compose.dev.yml # Override for dev container
└── Dockerfile             # Development container image

docker/
├── Dockerfile.api.dev     # API development image
├── Dockerfile.web.dev     # Web development image
└── docker-compose.deploy.yml # Production deployment

docker-compose.yml         # Base services (PostgreSQL, Redis)
docker-compose.dev.yml     # Full development stack
```

---

## 🔧 Available Scripts

From inside the dev container (or your host if you have pnpm):

```bash
# Development
pnpm dev              # Start web + api together (hot reload)

# Database
pnpm db:generate      # Generate Prisma client
pnpm db:push          # Push schema to database
pnpm db:seed          # Seed database with sample data
pnpm db:studio        # Open Prisma Studio

# Docker (run from host)
pnpm docker:local     # Start only PostgreSQL + Redis for local dev
pnpm docker:dev       # Start full development stack
pnpm docker:dev:logs  # View all container logs
pnpm docker:dev:build # Rebuild containers
pnpm docker:dev:reset # Full reset with volume cleanup
```

---

## 🔄 Hot Reload

Both options support hot-reload:
- **API (NestJS)**: Changes to `apps/api/src` auto-reload the server
- **Web (Next.js)**: Changes to `apps/web/src` auto-reload the browser
- **Shared packages**: Changes to `packages/*` trigger rebuilds

---

## 🗄️ Database Access

**Connection string:**
```
postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/nucleus
```

**Via Prisma Studio:**
```bash
# Inside dev container or with local pnpm
pnpm db:studio
```

**Via psql (if you have it locally):**
```bash
docker exec -it nucleus-postgres psql -U postgres -d nucleus
```

---

## 🧹 Cleanup

```bash
# Stop and remove containers (keeps data)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down

# Stop and remove containers + volumes (deletes data)
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down -v

# Remove all project images
docker rmi $(docker images 'nucleus*' -q)
```

---

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

### Hot reload not working?
- Make sure `WATCHPACK_POLLING=true` is set (it's in the docker-compose)
- Try rebuilding: `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build`

### Port already in use?
- Check what's using the port: `lsof -i :3000`
- Stop the conflicting service or change the port in docker-compose.dev.yml

### Container won't start?
- Check logs: `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs api`
- Try a full reset: `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml down -v`

### Permission issues?
- The containers run as non-root user for security
- Make sure your source files have correct permissions

```


</details>
</tool-use>

- Clarified `pnpm dev` (runs web + api with hot reload).
- Clarified `pnpm docker:local` (starts only PostgreSQL + Redis for local dev). See `undefined`.

About starting `pnpm dev`:
- Please rebuild/reopen the Dev Container first (`F1` → Dev Containers: Rebuild Container).
- Once you’re inside the container terminal, I can run `pnpm dev` for you—just confirm when the container is ready.

This is happening because during the build phase, Docker Compose doesn't see postgres/redis since they have `profiles: ["local"]`. The app service depends on them but they're not being included in the build.

The issue is that the override is removing the profile requirement with `profiles: []` but that's not working correctly during build time.

The solution is to either:
1. Remove the dependency on postgres/redis during build (only needed at runtime)
2. Use a different approach where we don't rely on compose profiles

I think the cleanest solution is to use the `build` key in devcontainer.json instead of dockerComposeFile when we need a custom Dockerfile. Or we simplify by removing the depends_on during build.

The user is still having permission issues with pnpm. The problem is that the volume for node_modules is being mounted at /workspace/node_modules, but pnpm is trying to create a store at /workspace/.pnpm-store which might be owned by root because of how the volume mount works.

Let me think about this more carefully:

1. The workspace is mounted as `..:/workspace:cached` - this mounts the host directory
2. node_modules is a named volume mounted at `/workspace/node_modules`
3. pnpm_store is now mounted at `/home/node/.pnpm-store`

But the issue is that pnpm by default wants to use `/workspace/.pnpm-store` based on the project location. We need to configure pnpm to use a different store location.

Let me check the .npmrc or create one to set the store directory. Also, the workspace folder itself might have permission issues since it's mounted from the host.

Actually, let me take a different approach - run the container as root to avoid all these permission issues during development. This is a dev container, not production. Or we could fix the permissions properly.

Let me try a simpler approach: use root user for development to avoid permission complexity.Let me simplify this - run as root to avoid all permission complexity (this is just for development): Now update `devcontainer.json` to use root user: Also update docker-compose to use root pnpm store: Now rebuild the container. 1. Container runs as `root` (avoids all permission issues)

```
[2026-01-04T15:41:43.239Z] Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/1_COMPANY_DEV/CompanyApps/Nucleus-Business-Suite/.devcontainer/.devcontainer/Dockerfile'
```

The issue is that the build context is being set to `.devcontainer` directory, and then the Dockerfile path is `.devcontainer/Dockerfile`, which results in looking for `.devcontainer/.devcontainer/Dockerfile`.

Looking at the compose config output:
```
build:
  context: /home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/1_COMPANY_DEV/CompanyApps/Nucleus-Business-Suite/.devcontainer
  dockerfile: .devcontainer/Dockerfile
```

The context is `.devcontainer` but the dockerfile path should just be `Dockerfile` (since it's relative to the context).

Since I'm using `${localWorkspaceFolder:-.}` which resolves to the `.devcontainer` folder when running from devcontainer.json, I need to fix the dockerfile path.

Let me fix this - the dockerfile should just be `Dockerfile` when context is the devcontainer folder, or I need to set context to the parent and dockerfile to `.devcontainer/Dockerfile`.

The cleaner approach: set context to parent directory (`..`) and dockerfile to `.devcontainer/Dockerfile`.I see the issue! The build context resolves to `.devcontainer` directory, but the Dockerfile path is also `Dockerfile`, resulting in looking for `Dockerfile`. Let me fix this: Now try rebuilding the Dev Container again. The fix:
- **Context**: `..` (parent directory = project root)
- **Dockerfile**: `Dockerfile` (relative to project root)

This ensures the path resolves correctly to `Dockerfile` instead of `Dockerfile`.
